key_insight: The LLMSurvival framework enables effective censoring-aware survival analysis using large language models on tabular clinical data.
engineering_relevance: This approach enhances survival prediction methods in medical settings, potentially improving patient outcomes.
method: Reformulates time-to-event prediction as pairwise ranking among subjects.
Derives test-time risk by aggregating comparisons against anchor individuals.
Utilizes unmodified LLMs for handling complex clinical datasets.
evidence_signal: LLMSurvival outperforms Cox proportional hazards modeling and established deep learning models in clinical tasks.
watch_item: The framework's dependence on the quality and representativeness of training data could impact its performance.
paper_title: Towards end-to-end LLM-based censoring-aware survival analysis
